Real-time high-rate seismogeodesy using Galileo HAS and BDS
PPP-B2b products: a shake table experiment and application to the
2024 M 7.0 Wushi earthquake

Among satellite-based PPP services, the BeiDou Navigation Satellite System (BDS-3) began providing the PPP-B2b augmentation
service on 2020 July 31, broadcasting State Space Representation (SSR) corrections for BDS-3 and GPS via GEO satellites, covering
China and surrounding regions (Y. Yang et al . 2022 ). On 2023 January 24, Galileo launched the High Accuracy Service (HAS), broad-
casting corrections for GPS and Galileo through the E6B sig

Figure 1 The experimental equipment with the Quanser Shake Table II and Trimble Zephyr geodetic model 2 antenna.
Figure 2 The distribution of monitoring stations (circles), earthquake epicentres (star) and the Maidan–Shayilamu Fault (lines).
2.1 Shake table experiment data
The Quanser Shake Table II (Fig. 1 ) was used to simulate real seismic waveforms. It is a medium-sized single-axis shake table capable
of generating actual earthquake signals with a sampling frequency of 2000 Hz and a positioning resolution of 3.10 μm. A Trimble
Zephyr geodeti

4 R.Fangetal.
3 METHODOLOGY
This section first describes the procedures for recovering precise satellite orbit and clock products from the PPP-B2b and HAS correc-
tions, then outlines the integration method for combining PPP-B2b and HAS products. Finally, the PPP processing strategies based on
the augmentation products are presented.
3.1 Precise orbit and clock recovery method
Both PPP-B2b and HAS products adopt the SSR model and provide orbit and clock corrections relative to broadcast ephemerides.
Therefore, it is necessary to apply these corrections to broadcast ephemerides before the produ

Figure 15 The PGD values and PGD magnitudes derived from PPP using the PPP-B2b, HAS and their integrated products, with the WUM product
serving as a reference.
Fig. 15 illustrates the extracted PGD values and the corresponding PGD magnitudes derived from different products. The PGD
values vary among stations due to differences in hypocentral distance. However, for individual stations, the differences among the
various products are relatively small, although the HAS product exhibits slightly poorer performance. To provide an overall comparison,
the mean PGD magnitude across the s

Real-timehigh-rateseismogeodesyusingGalileoHAS 15
product. Overall, these results demonstrate that PPP-B2b and HAS, especially when integrated, can provide high-precision real-time
positioning, offering strong potential for seismogeodetic applications in regions with limited communication infrastructure.
